Purpose: Retracing the whole process of recombination and coalescence along a genome
Specifics: Provides quantitative estimates for TMRCA and topologies at each locus. ARGWeaver-D can estimate introgression. Estimates effective population size. Provides tools to extract summary statistics for the topologies retrieved. Does not require phasing (but slower).
Issues and warnings: High computing cost. Slower on unphased or low depth data. ARGWeaver-D is not part of the Anaconda (Python) distribution (http://compgen.cshl.edu/ARGweaver/doc/argweaver-d-manual.html)
Link: Can be installed via conda: conda install -c genomedk argweaver and https://github.com/mjhubisz/argweaver and http://compgen.cshl.edu/ARGweaver/doc/argweaver-d-manual.html

Purpose: Detecting association with environmental/phenotypical features
Specifics: Includes most methods for GWAS studies, including procedures for fast computation, mixed linear models, efficient mixed model association, bayesian methods such as BLINK, diagnostics such as QQ plots and genotype filtering.
Issues and warnings: May be slow for very large datasets

Purpose: Detecting association with environmental/phenotypical features
Specifics: Computationnally efficient for large scale datasets
Issues and warnings: Imports data from PLINK format

Purpose: Detecting association with environmental/phenotypic features
Specifics: Modularity, facilitates correction for population structure/relatedness.
Issues and warnings: Imports data from PLINK format. No longer supported!

Purpose: Detecting association with environmental/phenotypical features
Specifics: Handles a variety of tests for population structure and relatedness
Issues and warnings: Population structure/kinship need to be assessed prior association analysis

Purpose: Detecting association with environmental/phenotypical features
Specifics: Specifically designed to handle categorical variables with more than 2 categories. Performs multinomial logistic regression and provides frequentist and bayesian frameworks.
Issues and warnings: Requires lapack library in Unix. Allows fine-mapping by testing for corrrelations between adjacent markers.

Purpose: Detecting association with environmental/phenotypical features
Specifics: Designed to be fast, underlying models have been kept simple. Allows conversion from PLINK format. Takes into account spatial autocorrelation of individual genotypes. Allows correction for population structure
Issues and warnings: Does not work with pooled data. Possibly high levels of false positives. Relatedness between samples should be assessed independently. Should be used in combination with LFMM or BayPass.

Purpose: Reconstruct genome-wide genealogies for hundreds of samples
Specifics: Provides quantitative estimates for TMRCA and topologies at each locus. Infers past demography (similar to PSMC methods). Infers changes in mutation rates. Performs scans for positive selection over discrete time periods.
Issues and warnings: Requires an outgroup to polarize alleles as ancestral/derived. Requires a recombination map. Does not reconstruct ARG sensus stricto, and does not estimate uncertainty of the local genealogies

Purpose: Predicting IBD tracts from demographic models
Specifics: High IBD sharing suggests recent positive selection.
Issues and warnings: Uses diCal output to obtain expectations based on demographic scenarios

Purpose: Adaptive introgression
Specifics: Detects a specific signature of increase then drop in diversity near a selected locus brought in a population through introgression
Issues and warnings: Private input format. Computationnally intensive, needs to be run in parallel.

Purpose: Detecting positive selection
Specifics: Designed for detecting recent positive selection. Clains to be more precise at identifying selected sites
Issues and warnings: The ancestral state of alleles must be obtained through an outgroup

Purpose: Detecting adaptation to environmental features
Specifics: Corrects for population structure using latent factors, faster than BAYENV for large datasets
Issues and warnings: Only performs association with environment

Purpose: Estimate the time at which a beneficial allele rises in frequency
Specifics: Previous version used ARGWeaver output, current version uses Relate. Provides scripts to plot the trajectory of selected alleles.
Issues and warnings: Assumes a panmictic population, neglects the effects of selection at linked sites.

Purpose: Estimate the strength and timing of selection on polygenic traits
Specifics: Uses genealogies estimated from Relate and results from GWAS to estimate timing and strength of selection for polygenic traits. Should be robust to pleiotropy and residual structure in GWAS
Issues and warnings: May overestimate selection for older events. Only tested in humans.

Purpose: Estimate the time at which a beneficial allele rises in frequency
Specifics: Compares genealogies between carriers and non-carriers of an advantageous mutation, assuming a star- genealogy at selected loci. Can handle VCF files
Issues and warnings: Requires a reference panel of noncarrier haplotypes. Sensitive to loca diversity before the sweep, and to migration events during a sweep. More indicated for recent sweeps.

Purpose: Adaptive introgression
Specifics: Estimates the selective coefficient of the introgressed loci through a hidden-Markov chain approach.
Issues and warnings: Requires the time and extent of introgression to be defined by the user

Purpose: Detecting selection using signatures of high LD
Specifics: Does not require phased data. Designed for detecting soft sweeps
Issues and warnings: Coalescent simulations are recommended to evaluate the likelihood of selection

Purpose: Detecting selection using signatures of high LD
Specifics: Can be used to address population structure or detect large inversions or indel polymorphism through LD
Issues and warnings: The user needs to play with parameters to ensure robustness of SNPs significantly linked

Purpose: Detecting selection using signatures of high LD
Specifics: R package. Computes EHH, iHS, XP-EHH, XP-nSL, and Rsb genome-wide. Accepts VCF directly and standardises iHS by allele-frequency bin. The most convenient haplotype-statistics implementation alongside selscan.
Issues and warnings: Requires phased data and high marker density. Standardisation depends on the choice of frequency bins. Not robust to background selection on its own - pair with demographic-aware methods.

Software: Scan for epistatic interaction (based on LD)
Purpose: Polygenic selection/Epistatic interactions
Specifics: Uses genome-wide LD between a candidate locus and the rest of the genomes to identify epistatic interactions. Can test SNP-SNP interaction, or between genomic windows (summarizes genotypes through PCA)
Issues and warnings: Lack of a detailed tutorial

Purpose: Detecting selection using signatures of high LD
Specifics: Includes the nSL statistics dedicated to soft sweep detection
Issues and warnings: Does not include utilities to specify the ancestral state of alleles. Requires phased data and high density of markers

Purpose: Detecting balancing selection
Specifics: Designed for detecting ancient balancing selection. Does not require phasing
Issues and warnings: Requires whole-genome data and recombination map. The ancestral state of alleles must be obtained through an outgroup

Purpose: Detecting balancing selection
Specifics: Uses correlations in frequencies between genomically proximate SNPs to compute a score. Can incorporate information about ancestral/derived alleles, fixed derived variants and normalizes the statistics depending on the amount of sites in a given genomic window. Very detailed tutorial and utilities.
Issues and warnings: Requires estimating the length distribution of ancestral fragments on each side of the selected site. The 95% percentile can be estimated with the formula L=-log(0.05)/(T*rho), with T the time since selection in generations and rho the effective recombination rate/ generation.

Purpose: Detecting balancing selection
Specifics: Examines the observed and expected frequency spectra of polymorphisms in genomic windows to test for selection. Can incorporate fixed differences with an outgroup (NCD2), but not mandatory (NCD1)
Issues and warnings: Private input format, requires simulations to calibrate the statistics. Requires to define the expected equilibrium frequency of alleles (usually between 0.3 and 0.5). Low sensitivity below these frequencies.

Purpose: Detecting positive selection and local adaptation
Specifics: Incorporates uncertainty on allele frequencies due to low sample sizes
Issues and warnings: Sensitive to priors on the ratio of selected/neutral sites. False positive rates can be high under scenarios of demographic expansion, admixture and isolation by distance

Purpose: Detecting positive selection and local adaptation
Specifics: Allows to control for hierarchical population structure
Issues and warnings: False positive rate is high when an island model cannot be assumed

Purpose: Detecting positive selection and local adaptation
Specifics: R package. Detects loci that contribute disproportionately to population structure (PCA-based outlier detection); does not require predefined populations. Handles admixed and Pool-Seq data. Often paired with environmental-association tools (LFMM, BayPass C2) to sharpen the candidate list.
Issues and warnings: False-positive rate can be high without empirical FDR control. Sensitive to the chosen number of PCs - inspect the scree plot and use multiple K values.

Purpose: Detecting positive selection and local adaptation
Specifics: Can estimate the coefficients of selection. Calibration using a pseudo-observed dataset (can be used in combination with the R function simulate.baypass() in BayPass).
Issues and warnings: Assumes a Wrigth-Fisher island model.

Purpose: Detecting positive selection and adaptation to environmental features
Specifics: Less sensitive to demographic history than FST-based methods. Handles pooled (Pool-Seq) and individual genotype data. Recent versions implement the C2 contrast statistic (Olazcuaga et al. 2020) to test for adaptive divergence between groups of populations defined by a binary covariate (native vs. invasive, host A vs. host B, etc.) while controlling for the neutral covariance structure. Often paired with the local-score approach to combine SNP-level signals.
Issues and warnings: Significance thresholds need to be calibrated with pseudo-observed datasets (provided by the simulate.baypass() routine). Calibration with neutral SNPs is recommended. The C2 contrast requires a meaningful binary covariate. BayPass better estimates the kinship matrix than Bayenv.

Purpose: Detecting positive selection and local adaptation
Specifics: Less sensitive to population demographic history than previous methods
Issues and warnings: Requires an outgroup population

Purpose: Detecting positive selection and local adaptation
Specifics: Compares the level of exclusively shared differences between internal and external branches of a population tree. Allows testing selection occurring on the ancestral branch leading to two populations.
Issues and warnings: Requires several populations to perform the test. May be less sensitive to selection on standing variation.

Purpose: Detecting selection using AFS, differentiation
Specifics: Extracts summary statistics directly from BAM files
Issues and warnings: Does not allow for sophisticated filtering and SNP calling

Purpose: Detecting selection using AFS, differentiation
Specifics: Extracts summary statistics from VCF files. Also allows VCF filtering and conversion
Issues and warnings: Set of summary statistics not as extensive as PopGenome

Purpose: Detecting positive selection and local adaptation
Specifics: Scans the genome for composite signals of selective sweeps summarized by the μ statistics. Corrects for the effects of background selection by estimating a threshold value for the statistics based on simulations with background selection
Issues and warnings: Uses a single population of interest.

Purpose: Detecting association with phenotype
Specifics: User friendly (Java interface), corrects for relatedness, allows computing summary statistics (LD, diversity)
Issues and warnings: Requires relatedness to be assessed externally (with e.g. STRUCTURE)

Purpose: Detecting selection using AFS, differentiation, association with functional traits
Specifics: Allows for association using generalized linear models
Issues and warnings: Descriptive statistics. P-values need to be evaluated through coalescent simulations.

Purpose: Designed for whole genome data (or large continuous regions)
Specifics: Supports Fasta and VCF formats. Estimates selection coefficients.
Issues and warnings: NA

Purpose: Detecting selection using AFS, differentiation and LD statistics
Specifics: Allows combining several tools in a single pipeline. Includes phasing tools.
Issues and warnings: Set of available summary statistics remains limited (same as VCFtools + Fay and Wu's H)

Purpose: Distribution of fitness effects/selection on coding variation
Specifics: Estimates selection along branches in a phylogeny for genes of interest, contrasting patterns of synonymous and non-synonymous substitutions. A detailed tutorial is available here: https://link.springer.com/protocol/10.1007%2F978-1-4939-1438-8_4#Sec29
Issues and warnings: Slow for large datasets. Needs to be parallelised.

Purpose: Distribution of fitness effects/selection on coding variation
Specifics: Can test for invariance of DFEs across datasets (genomic regions within species, or different species). No need for divergence estimates (does not assume that the same DFE is shared between species and outgroup). Very detailed tutorial available here: https://link.springer.com/protocol/10.1007/978-1-0716-0199-0_6
Issues and warnings: Comparisons require a large number of SNPs for each dataset for comparisons to be meaningful

Purpose: Detecting selection using AFS, differentiation
Specifics: Fast, embedded in R, allows using annotation files (GFF/GTF format).
Issues and warnings: Does not perform association, but can be used in combination with GENABEL within R

Purpose: Detect cryptic ghost-lineage introgression in four-taxon genomic datasets
Specifics: Extends the D-statistic / four-taxon framework to flag introgression from unsampled ('ghost') lineages, which are systematically misattributed by classical D / f4 tests. Useful complement to Sprime / hmmix when only a few taxa are available.
Issues and warnings: Preprint stage; benchmarking outside test simulations is ongoing. Like all four-taxon tests, sensitive to ancestral population structure.

Purpose: Reproducible CNN sweep classification with stdpopsim-derived training
Specifics: Composite workflows wrap diploS/HIC, partialS/HIC and Flex-sweep with stdpopsim demographic priors so demographic-mis-specification risk is reduced. Recommended over running any single ML sweep classifier in isolation.
Issues and warnings: Heavy compute for training; cross-validation on real data is essential before publication.

Purpose: Detect natural selection on transposable elements while accounting for TE age
Specifics: Novel SFS-based statistic that incorporates the age of TE insertions; designed for long-read assemblies of multiple individuals. Improves resolution over standard SFS-based selection tests when applied to TEs.
Issues and warnings: Requires high-quality long-read assemblies for complex genomes and accurate TE age estimates; benchmarked on maize. Preprint stage.

Purpose: Detect balancing selection allowing variable footprint size; multi-allelic and incomplete-sweep extensions
Specifics: Composite likelihood ratio mixture model on B0/B1/B2 statistics. BalLeRMix+ extends to deep-time and ML-aided detection.
Issues and warnings: Demographic mis-specification and recombination-rate variation inflate false positives; requires matched neutral simulations.

Purpose: Composite-likelihood detection of ancient balancing selection using SFS and substitutions
Specifics: T2 incorporates outgroup substitutions; underlies BALLET. Often complementary to BetaScan and NCD.
Issues and warnings: Sensitive to outgroup choice and demographic mis-specification.

Purpose: Fine-mapping the favoured allele within a sweep region
Specifics: Coalescent-aware ranking of candidate variants; complementary to iHS/nSL/SweepFinder which find regions but not specific causal SNPs.
Issues and warnings: Requires phased data; assumes a (near-)complete classic sweep.

Purpose: Deep-learning classification of genomic windows into hard sweep / soft sweep / linked / neutral
Specifics: CNN trained on simulated data. partialS/HIC handles partial / incomplete sweeps. Often paired with stdpopsim simulations.
Issues and warnings: Strong dependence on training simulation realism; out-of-distribution misclassification when demography mismatches.

Purpose: Deep-learning detection of sweeps across a wide range of strengths, ages and starting frequencies
Specifics: CNN trained with stdpopsim demography; extends diploS/HIC to older / softer sweeps.
Issues and warnings: Heavy training compute; demographic mis-specification still problematic.

Purpose: Deep-learning inference of selection from inferred ARGs / genealogies
Specifics: Operates on Relate output to detect and date sweeps; bridges ARG-inference and selection scans.
Issues and warnings: Inherits errors from upstream ARG inference; benchmarking outside humans is limited.

Purpose: Estimate allele-frequency trajectories and selection coefficients from inferred ARGs
Specifics: Successor of CLUES; works on Relate or tsinfer/tsdate genealogies; estimates s and time of onset of selection.
Issues and warnings: ARG quality is the dominant source of error; requires demographic model.

Purpose: Deep-learning extension of the mu-statistic sweep scan
Specifics: Combines summary-statistic detection with neural-network classification; very fast on whole genomes.
Issues and warnings: Training-set demography critical; can flag background-selection regions as sweeps.

Purpose: Composite-likelihood detection of completed selective sweeps
Specifics: SweepFinder2 adds robustness to background selection and can incorporate substitutions; OmegaPlus uses LD patterns.
Issues and warnings: Both assume completed classic sweeps; demographic mis-specification inflates false positives.

Software: HyPhy (BUSTED, aBSREL, RELAX, FUBAR, MEME, FEL)
Purpose: Codon-based tests of episodic, branch-specific, lineage-specific or site-specific selection
Specifics: Standard for testing positive/negative selection on protein-coding genes; runs locally or via Datamonkey.
Issues and warnings: Requires reliable codon alignment; sensitive to recombination within genes (use GARD first).

Purpose: Estimate the distribution of fitness effects (DFE) and the proportion / rate of adaptive substitutions (alpha, omega_a)
Specifics: Folded/unfolded SFS-based extension of MK approaches; widely used in non-model species.
Issues and warnings: Sensitive to demography (account for via SFS shape priors) and to mutation-rate heterogeneity.

Purpose: Joint estimation of the DFE and the rate of adaptive evolution
Specifics: SFS-based; widely used reference implementation alongside polyDFE and Grapes.
Issues and warnings: Assumes independence among sites; sensitive to demographic priors.

Purpose: Demography-aware fine-scale recombination rate estimation from LD
Specifics: Likelihood method using composite likelihoods that account for inferred demographic history; faster and more accurate than LDhat under realistic demographies.
Issues and warnings: Requires a precomputed demographic history (e.g., from Stairway2/SMC++).

Purpose: Recombination rate estimation along the genome using a recurrent neural network
Specifics: Trained on simulated data; outputs per-window rho with uncertainty.
Issues and warnings: Sensitive to training-set demography; does not handle structural variants well.

Purpose: Pipeline-style sweep scan combining SFS and haplotype statistics with ML classification
Specifics: Recent open-source pipelines wrapping multiple statistics under a unified, demography-aware framework.
Issues and warnings: Often demography-specific; check if maintained.

Purpose: Detect archaic / divergent introgression from a single ingroup population without an archaic reference
Specifics: S-prime statistic identifies long, divergent haplotypes; widely used for archaic introgression.
Issues and warnings: Needs an unadmixed outgroup; calibrated mainly on human data.

Purpose: Detect introgressed haplotypes without using the donor reference
Specifics: Original 'reference-free' archaic introgression statistic; precursor of Sprime.
Issues and warnings: Sensitive to demographic mis-specification; needs careful null calibration.

Purpose: Detect introgressed segments using IBD between modern and archaic genomes
Specifics: Does not require an unadmixed reference, allowing detection of low-level archaic ancestry in African populations.
Issues and warnings: Requires high-quality archaic genome; sensitive to phasing.

Purpose: Reference-free detection of archaic introgression segments
Specifics: HMM trained on outgroup-derived variants; widely used in non-African modern human studies and useful when archaic genomes are unavailable.
Issues and warnings: Sensitive to outgroup choice and to recent admixture from unmodelled sources.

Purpose: Detect and date archaic introgression tracts
Specifics: HMM with explicit demographic prior; estimates introgression times.
Issues and warnings: Depends on the assumed demography; mainly developed for human data.

Purpose: Local-ancestry inference from low-coverage and ancient DNA, including archaic ancestry
Specifics: HMM that explicitly handles low-coverage / contaminated data, including aDNA.
Issues and warnings: Performance depends on coverage and on reference-allele frequency calibration.

Purpose: Detect archaic introgression with a logistic-regression / ML classifier on simulated training data
Specifics: Reference-free; trained on simulations; one of the early supervised-learning introgression detectors.
Issues and warnings: Strong dependence on training-simulation realism; benchmarks show lower agreement with reference-aware methods.

Purpose: Detect adaptive introgression with an ML classifier
Specifics: Random-forest / NN trained on simulated data; tailored to adaptive (rather than neutral) introgression.
Issues and warnings: Relies on training-set assumptions; out-of-distribution false positives common.

Purpose: Semantic segmentation of introgressed loci with a U-Net
Specifics: Treats introgression detection as image segmentation across a haplotype matrix; high resolution at the variant level.
Issues and warnings: Heavy training requirements; demographic mis-specification reduces accuracy.

Purpose: Deep-learning detection of introgression for arbitrary species (not only human-archaic)
Specifics: CNN architecture with explicit handling of unphased data; benchmarked beyond humans.
Issues and warnings: Training dataset must match target taxa; calibration recommended on real data.

Purpose: Detect introgression from gene-tree branch-length distributions
Specifics: Mixture model on internal-branch lengths of gene trees; complementary to D-statistic methods at deeper time scales.
Issues and warnings: Requires reliable gene trees; sensitive to among-lineage rate variation.

Purpose: Bayesian inference and quantification of introgression with conjugate priors
Specifics: Provides credible intervals on f-statistics-like quantities; relatively new and explicitly Bayesian.
Issues and warnings: Newer tool — benchmarking in non-human systems is limited.

Purpose: Topology-weighting analysis along the genome
Specifics: Successor of Twisst; faster and more flexible; useful for complementing D-statistics with phylogenetic signal.
Issues and warnings: Requires reliable local trees; output interpretation depends on sampling scheme.

Purpose: Detect adaptive introgression candidates with U and Q summary statistics
Specifics: Window-based statistics looking for high-frequency variants in the recipient that are rare in non-introgressed populations and present in the donor.
Issues and warnings: Requires donor reference and an unadmixed outgroup; thresholds need calibration.
Reference: (Racimo et al., 2017; Huang et al., 2025 review)

Purpose: Bayesian multispecies coalescent inference of species delimitation, divergence and introgression
Specifics: Now supports MSci (multispecies coalescent with introgression) models; canonical for closely related species.
Issues and warnings: Computationally intensive; prior choice has strong influence on outcomes.

Purpose: Inference of phylogenetic networks (reticulation events) from gene trees or sequence data
Specifics: Network-based alternative to D-statistics; estimates number and placement of reticulation edges.
Issues and warnings: Sensitive to gene-tree estimation error; computational cost grows fast with reticulations.
